legacy.full_text:
Using the X API to source leads.

I’ve had a few people ask how I found so many people in tech who also happened to be cracked at games for this LAN I’m throwing. My network helped, but a lot of it came from getting creative with the X API.

TLDR:

I used @Scobleizer’s tech lists https://t.co/raJIKX7Jxt

result.text:
Using the X API to source leads.

I’ve had a few people ask how I found so many people in tech who also happened to be cracked at games for this LAN I’m throwing. My network helped, but a lot of it came from getting creative with the X API.

TLDR:

I used @Scobleizer’s tech lists as a starting pool, searched them for anyone who had posted about League of Legends, Valorant, TFT, chess, or Clash Royale, had agents read the results then filter out all candidates that were clearly not near SF.

That turned into

17,683 possible posts → 4,888 real gaming posts → 1,184 people → 647 after location filtering.

Key learnings (written by Codex):

- The list: operator is a cheat code. Instead of downloading 160,000+ overlapping List memberships and searching every profile individually, I could run:
list:<LIST_ID> (<GAME_TERMS>) -is:retweet
X handled the List membership part for me and only returned potentially relevant Posts.

- Don’t use the X website to map Lists. The page only showed me 35 at first because of how it loads while scrolling. The API returned all 66 Lists with their real IDs immediately.

- Search wide, filter hard. Terms like league, TFT, and chess surfaced a ton of nonsense: sports leagues, thin-film transistors, AI latency, and “4D chess” metaphors. Almost 13,000 Posts were rejected after the agents read them.

- Don’t fetch everything upfront. I waited until a Post looked promising before fetching its quoted context or the author’s full profile. That turned thousands of possible follow-up calls into 15 batches for Post context and 18 for profiles.

- Parallel agents are great, but check their work. I split the Posts into batches, had multiple agents review them, and validated that every Post received exactly one decision. Anything uncertain went through independent review instead of being guessed.

- Go wide without brute force. We searched all 66 Lists end-to-end and reviewed every hit, giving us broad coverage without individually crawling thousands of profiles.

legacy.full_text:
i built a Grok/X-powered agent swarm that simulates presidential elections between anyone you want: https://t.co/J8mU99fIfa

simply give agents the names of the candidates, their running mates, and the "state of the union," then 500+ agents pull data from X using the X MCP to

result.text:
i built a Grok/X-powered agent swarm that simulates presidential elections between anyone you want: https://t.co/vxu35CSazG

simply give agents the names of the candidates, their running mates, and the "state of the union," then 500+ agents pull data from X using the X MCP to deliberate and vote on the best candidate.

🇺🇸 The American Experiment — 2028
Taylor Swift vs Charlie Kirk
🔵 Taylor Swift 309 ✓
🔴 Charlie Kirk 229
🗳️ Popular vote 50.6%–49.4%
https://t.co/wnEmHdHFdC

more details on how i built it ->
